free_text: Energy levels of O-17 from C-13(alpha,alpha)C-13 and
C-13(alpha,n)O-16
12 MeV tandem Van de Graaff
Last checking has been done
On. Major revision in 004.
SD: Corrections in several Subents.
Subent 005 added.
The targets were thin self-supporting (20-30 ug/cm**2)
foils, made by cracking C-13 enriched methyl iodide
onto nickel backing.
Two surface-barrier detectors.
All measurements were corrected for build-up of C-12
target. The build-up, which was assumed to have occured
uniformly over any one run, varied considerably from
run to run.  The maximum correction required was 30%
with typical corrections of about 10% after an
eight-hour run.
The data were taken from Sigma Data-Base
SD:STATUS updated. ERR-ANALYS -> CORRECTION
Enriched targets of C-13 of 20-30 mug/cm**2 were
prepared by cracking enriched methyl iodide directly
onto a Ta strip.
The (alpha,n) measurements were made in a thin-walled
brass chamber suspended so as to reduce the amount of
scattering material in the vicinity of the chamber and
detectors.
Two 2.5 cm * 5 cm plastic scintillators.
A BF3 long-counter and a 7.6 cm * 7.6 cm plastic
scintillator served as monitors for the angular
distribution measurements.
By CAJAD from fig.4 of Nucl.Phys.,A110(1968)637
SD: STATUS, ERR-ANALYS updated.
Enriched 13C targets (20-30 ug/cm2 thick) prepared by
cracking enriched methyl iodide directly onto a Ta
strip
Two 2.5 cmx5 cm plastic scintillators
BF3 long-counter as monitor
Plastic scintillator (7.6 cmx7.6 cm) as monitor
Corrected for 6.14 MeV gamma-ray background originated
from 13C(a,n'g)16O reaction above 5 MeV
No information on the source of uncertainty
Table 2 of Nucl.Phys.A110(1968)637
REACTION(SF8): LEG/RSD -> LEG/RS.
Data (sign) corrected at 5.46(L=4) and 5.70(L=3) MeV.
The targets were thin self-supporting (20-30 ug/cm**2)
foils, made by cracking C-13 enriched methyl iodide
onto nickel backing.
Two surface-barrier detectors.
All measurements were corrected for build-up of C-12
target. The build-up,which was assumed to have occured
uniformly over any one run, varied considerably from
run to run.  The maximum correction required was 30%
with typical corrections of about 10% after an
eight-hour run.
Fig.3 from Nucl.Phys.,A110(1968)637
